commit.message:
Merge 1.21.0 back to dev (#1738)

## PR Title Format

**Required Format:** `[Keyword1] [Keyword2]: Description`

- **Keyword1:** `major`, `minor`, or `patch` (case-insensitive)
- **Keyword2:** `feature`, `bugfix`, `engg`, or `tests`
(case-insensitive)

**Examples:**
- `[MAJOR] [Feature]: new API`
- `[minor] [bugfix]: fix crash`
- `[PATCH][tests]:add coverage`

## Proposed changes

Merge 1.21.0 back to dev

## Type of change

- [ ] Feature work
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Engineering change
- [ ] Test
- [ ] Logging/Telemetry

## Risk

- [ ] High – Errors could cause MAJOR regression of many scenarios.
(Example: new large features or high level infrastructure changes)
- [ ] Medium – Errors could cause regression of 1 or more scenarios.
(Example: somewhat complex bug fixes, small new features)
- [ ] Small – No issues are expected. (Example: Very small bug fixes,
string changes, or configuration settings changes)

## Additional information

---------

commit.message:
Add AI confidence scores across GPT JSON

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Persist matrix animation state across runs and align to terminal top (#343)

The matrix animation was reset on every `App::new()` and its coordinates
were viewport-relative, causing it to jump/restart between prompts
instead of appearing stationary against the terminal background.

## Changes

**State persistence**
- Removed `reset_matrix_anim_state()` call from `App::new()` — the
global `MATRIX_ANIM_STATE` now lives for the terminal session, not
per-prompt invocation
- Removed the now-dead `reset_matrix_anim_state` function

**Terminal-absolute coordinates**
- `apply_matrix_anim` now takes `viewport_top: u16` and
`terminal_height: u16`
- `state.update()` receives `terminal_height` so tendril `y`-positions
are in terminal-absolute rows (0 = terminal top)
- When writing tendrils into the content buffer, rows above
`viewport_top` are skipped via `.skip(viewport_top as usize)`; buffer
index = `term_row - viewport_top`

**Buffer sizing**
- Buffer is extended to `terminal_height - viewport_top` rows (fills
viewport-top → terminal-bottom), replacing the hardcoded `40`
- Early return when `viewport_top >= terminal_height` (no visible area)

**Call-site plumbing**
- `create_content` gains `viewport_top` and `terminal_height` params
- Run loop passes `frame_area.y` and `last_terminal_area.height`

fix: restore Tailwind CSS loading and add mobile hamburger menu (#65)

- Fix CSS reference in App.razor: css/input.css → css/app.css
  The Tailwind CLI compiles Styles/input.css to wwwroot/css/app.css,
  but the HTML was referencing the source file path which doesn't
  exist in wwwroot, causing zero CSS to load.

- Add mobile hamburger menu to NavMenuComponent
  The nav used hidden md:flex with no mobile fallback, making the
  entire navigation invisible on screens < 768px. Added a hamburger
  toggle button (visible on mobile only) and a dropdown panel with
  vertical nav links that auto-closes on navigation.

- Fix inaccurate text on Home page: 'on the left' → 'above'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve error 400 in dashboard data loading

- Change Dashboard from fetch-based to prop-based architecture
  * Remove local state management and axios calls
  * Receive facturas directly from App.js
  * Eliminate error 400 (missing 'cliente' query parameter)

- Add correoUsuario to demo facturas in Login
  * Ensures demo mode displays facturas correctly
  * Aligns with backend filter requirements

- Remove CRUD operation buttons from Dashboard table
  * Simplify Dashboard to presentation-only component
  * Move edit/delete actions to dedicated FacturaList component

The fix centralizes data management in App.js:
- Demo mode: Uses user.facturasDemo directly
- Real mode: Fetches with ?cliente=email query param
- Dashboard receives data as prop and re-renders on updates

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: accelerate SurfaceNormal(p) with BVH face identification (#265)

* perf: accelerate SurfaceNormal(p) with BVH face identification

Phase 1 – all-planar fast path:
  When fAllFacesPlanar is true, find the closest face by minimum
  gp_Pln::Distance and return the precomputed fb.outwardNormal.
  Eliminates all OCCT solver calls for planar solids (g4box, g4trap,
  g4trd, g4para) — expected improvement from ~100–165 ms to <1 ms.

Phase 2 – BVH face identification for curved solids:
  * Extend PointToMeshDistance to track the winning triangle index
    (myBestIndex) and expose it via BestIndex().
  * Build fTriangleFaceIdx in ComputeBounds() immediately after the
    BVH triangle set: for each face (in the same TopExp_Explorer order
    as fFaceBoundsCache) append the face-cache index once per triangle,
    giving an O(1) triangle→face lookup at query time.
  * Add BVHFindNearestFaceBounds(p): traverses the BVH in
    O(log N_triangles) and returns a pointer to the nearest FaceBounds.
  * SurfaceNormal(p) now calls BVHFindNearestFaceBounds then runs
    GeomAPI_ProjectPointOnSurf on the single identified face (instead
    of TryFindClosestFace which ran BRepExtrema on every candidate face)
    and uses TryGetOutwardNormal(fb.adaptor, ...) with the cached adaptor
    (fast overload, skipping BRepAdaptor_Surface construction).
  * Retains the original TryFindClosestFace path as a fallback when the
    BVH is unavailable.

Also documents both patterns in AGENTS.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: apply review feedback to SurfaceNormal BVH optimisation (#266)

* Initial plan

* perf: reserve fTriangleFaceIdx capacity and extract projectAndGetNormal lambda

Address two review feedback items from PR #265:

1. Reserve fTriangleFaceIdx before insert loop in ComputeBounds() using
   fTriangleSet->Size() to avoid repeated reallocations for heavily
   tessellated shapes.

2. Extract the shared "project point → get (u,v) → evaluate outward
   normal" sequence into a projectAndGetNormal lambda in SurfaceNormal(p),
   eliminating code duplication between the BVH path and fallback path.
   The lambda takes an optional BRepAdaptor_Surface* so the BVH path uses
   the pre-built cached adaptor (fast overload) while the fallback constructs
   one on the fly.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/eic/G4OCCT/sessions/0d19984e-3333-45bb-b663-a08495b4d781

commit.message:
Fix the accuracy issue by update celu formula based on spec (#33500)

### Details:
- The implementation didn't align with [ONNX
spec](https://onnx.ai/onnx/operators/onnx__Celu.html).
```
max(0,x) + min(0,alpha*(exp(x/alpha)-1))
```
 

### Tickets:
 - [CVS-178958](https://jira.devtools.intel.com/browse/CVS-178958)

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
TECHDEBT-35: Fix VultronEvent to carry rich domain objects

Replace the flat ID/type string fields on VultronEvent with 7 rich
VultronObject | None fields (object_, target, context, origin,
inner_object, inner_target, inner_context) that mirror the AS2 Activity
structure at the core layer.

Changes:
- events/base.py: Replace 14 flat fields with 7 VultronObject fields;
  add derived @property for all old names (backward compat); object_
  uses trailing underscore because 'object' is a Python built-in
- events/_mixins.py: Add rich 'foo' property alongside each 'foo_id'
  property; properties return self.object_, self.target, etc. with
  domain-typed hints under TYPE_CHECKING
- event subclasses (report, case, embargo, note, status, case_participant,
  actor): Remove explicit typed domain fields (report: VultronReport,
  case: VultronCase, etc.) — now provided by mixin properties
- extractor.py: Update _build_domain_kwargs() to use object_= instead of
  per-semantic keys; add _to_domain_obj() helper; remove all flat _id/_type
  kwargs from constructor call; import VultronObject
- test_report_use_cases.py: Update 3 event constructors to use object_=

All 988 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(release): harden media runtime and publish packaging

Why: ingest outputs could overwrite each other, audio/video capability checks drifted between runtime and providers, and the publish pipeline could fail lint or ship stale dist artifacts.

Why: this keeps the validated 0.2.1 release changes together and makes the npm publish path reproducible.

commit.message:
refactor: TECHDEBT-39 — remove _find_and_update_participant_rm wrapper (OPP-05)

Removed the module-level _find_and_update_participant_rm() wrapper from
vultron/core/behaviors/report/nodes.py. Both TransitionParticipantRMtoAccepted
and TransitionParticipantRMtoDeferred now call update_participant_rm_state()
directly, inlining the bool→Status conversion and exception handling.

Removed redundant local 'from vultron.core.states.rm import RM' imports
inside each update() method — RM is already imported at module level.

There is now exactly one implementation of the 'append a new participant RM
status' operation: update_participant_rm_state() in triggers/_helpers.py,
used by both BT nodes and trigger use cases (SvcEngageCaseUseCase,
SvcDeferCaseUseCase).

988 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Vultron

commit.message:
geometry: add BVH lower-bound tier to DistanceToIn(p) (#264)

* geometry: add BVH lower-bound tier to DistanceToIn(p)

DistanceToIn(p) had only two code paths:
  Tier-0: AABB lower bound (O(1)) — effective only when point is outside the AABB
  Fallback: ExactDistanceToIn — BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier + BRepExtrema_DistShapeShape
            per face (heavy Newton iteration, especially slow for ellipsoid surfaces)

This caused a 2237× G4↔OCCT timing ratio in benchmarks (vs 3.4× for DistanceToOut),
and an OCCT/Exact ratio of only 1.07× — the 'lower bound' was doing essentially
the same work as the exact computation.

DistanceToOut(p) already uses BVHLowerBoundDistance (O(log N_triangles)) as its
primary answer for mixed-geometry solids, falling back to exact only when the BVH
returns ∞. This commit mirrors that pattern for DistanceToIn(p):

  Tier-0: AABB lower bound     O(1)           — unchanged
  Tier-1: BVH lower bound      O(log N_tri)   — new: returns for outside points
  Fallback: ExactDistanceToIn                 — now only reached near the surface

BVHLowerBoundDistance = max(0, meshDist − fBVHDeflection) is a provably conservative
lower bound on the true surface distance for outside points; it is valid as a
safety-distance under-estimate as DistanceToIn(p) is used for safety queries.

fTriangleSet, fBVHDeflection, and BVHLowerBoundDistance are all pre-existing
infrastructure (no new data structures or header changes needed).

Expected impact: ellipsoid-15x10x20 DTI drops from ~7300 ms to single-digit ms;
all curved-surface fixtures benefit similarly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: document tiered DTI/DTO acceleration pattern in AGENTS.md

Add a clear rule describing the three-tier DistanceToIn/Out(p) acceleration
structure so future contributors do not inadvertently revert to O(N_faces ×
Newton) behaviour for curved solids.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update src/G4OCCTSolid.cc

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: TECHDEBT-34 — add EM state transition guards to embargo use cases

Three unguarded direct em_state = EM.ACTIVE assignments in vultron/core/
now have explicit machine guards:

- SvcEvaluateEmbargoUseCase (trigger-side): replaced direct assignment with
  EMAdapter + create_em_machine() + adapter.accept() pattern. MachineError
  raises VultronConflictError. Consistent with SvcProposeEmbargoUseCase
  and SvcTerminateEmbargoUseCase.

- AddEmbargoEventToCaseReceivedUseCase (receive-side): added
  is_valid_em_transition() check with WARNING log on non-standard
  transition; proceeds regardless (documented state-sync override).

- AcceptInviteToEmbargoOnCaseReceivedUseCase (receive-side): same pattern.

All rm_state=RM.XXX in core are constructor args for new status objects
(initial-state constructions, not transitions). The append_rm_state()
guard already enforces is_valid_rm_transition() for all RM mutation paths.

Added 4 tests (2 updated + 2 new warning tests + 1 conflict error test).
988 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Release v0.2.13

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix glob expansions with glob chars in directory components (#339)

`foo*/ba*` expanded to `/full/path/to/foobar/baz` instead of
`foobar/baz` because `PathPatternExpansion` split at the last `/`,
putting glob chars in the prefix. `strip_prefix("/cwd/foo*")` then
failed against `/cwd/foobar/baz`, falling through to return the absolute
path.

### Fix

Split the pattern at the last `/` **before** the first unescaped glob
metacharacter (`*`, `?`, `[`) instead of at the last `/` overall:

```
foo*/ba*       → prefix="",    rhs="foo*/ba*"     → foobar/baz
abc/foo*/ba*   → prefix="abc", rhs="foo*/ba*"     → abc/foobar/baz
$PWD/man       → prefix="$PWD", rhs="man"         → unchanged
```

### Test changes

- Added `foo/baz` and `abc/foo/baz` to the Docker test filesystem
- Added integration test cases for `foo*/ba*` and `abc/foo*/ba*`
- Updated three existing tests to account for the new `abc/` directory

Add dim+italic palette style for inline history metadata (#342)

The `#idx= time=` metadata appended to inline history suggestions used
`secondary_text()` (dim only), making it visually indistinguishable from
the suggestion text itself.

## Changes

- **`src/palette.rs`** — New `Palette::history_inline_meta()` style:
`DIM | ITALIC`
- **`src/app/mod.rs`** — Swap `secondary_text()` →
`history_inline_meta()` on the `extra_info_text` span in inline history
rendering

```rust
pub fn history_inline_meta() -> Style {
    Style::default()
        .add_modifier(Modifier::DIM)
        .add_modifier(Modifier::ITALIC)
}
```

Fix spelling, grammar, and capitalisation in README, source, and demo files (#341)

Fixes inconsistent capitalisation of "Bash", several spelling mistakes,
and minor grammar/formatting issues across the README, Rust source (clap
help text), tape demo files, and example scripts.

## Capitalisation
- `bash` → `Bash` in prose references to the shell (README feature list,
section headings, link text)
- `bash` → `Bash` in `src/lib.rs` clap doc comments (visible in `flyline
agent-mode --help`)
- `bash` → `Bash` in `tapes/demo_overview.tape` and
`tapes/demo_agent_mode.tape` (user-visible demo text)
- `bash` → `Bash` in `examples/agent_mode.sh` example system prompt
strings

## Spelling
- `Agen mode` → `Agent mode`
- `dats` → `data`
- `exteneded` → `extended`

## Grammar & formatting
- `setup completions` → `set up completions` (verb form)
- `call grep's … if it's setup` → `calls grep's … if it's set up`
- `mid way` → `midway`
- Missing spaces: `…Settings = 1])to` → `…) to` and `to\`Control` → `to
\`Control`

commit.message:
refactor: use LLM tool-calling (bind_tools) for gen_ai.tool.definitions

Replace direct execute_tool() calls with llm.bind_tools() + tool execution
loop. The LLM now receives tool schemas and decides which tools to call.
This produces gen_ai.tool.definitions on chat spans in App Insights.

- tools.py: use @tool decorator, accept JSON string for plan arg
- graph.py: _invoke_with_tools() sends tool definitions to the LLM,
  executes tool_calls from the response, feeds ToolMessages back
- tests: StubLLM supports bind_tools() and returns tool_calls

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix: Remove width/height attributes from elements for HTML semantic compliance The width/height attributes on elements within are not valid per HTML standard. These attributes should only be on the element to prevent layout shift. Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove traceparent from metadata to enable HTTP span nesting

When traceparent/tracestate were in metadata, the AzureAIOpenTelemetryTracer
extracted them via _extract_trace_headers() and called use_propagated_context(),
creating a NEW root context that bypassed the FastAPI HTTP span. Now the
callback inherits the OTel context from the FastAPI ASGI instrumentor,
so the HTTP request span becomes the tree root.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: Use rawComment.body in applySuggestionWithCopilot to exclude AI warning suffix

When applying a suggestion using AI for Copilot Code Review comments,
the rendered comment body included the "Copilot is powered by AI, so
mistakes are possible" suffix. Use rawComment.body instead to get the
original comment text without the display-only warning.

commit.message:
climbingTiles: use fail-safe fetchOverpass() for refresh (#6)

### Description

The climbing-tiles refresh was hardcoded to `maps.mail.ru` Overpass
instance, which is currently down.

Switches to `fetchOverpass()` which rotates through multiple servers
(`overpass.private.coffee` → `overpass-api.de` → `maps.mail.ru`) with
automatic retry on 429/5xx errors.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: zbycz <385047+zbycz@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use configure_azure_monitor() for reliable App Insights export

Replace manual TracerProvider + BatchSpanProcessor with the Azure Monitor
OpenTelemetry distro (configure_azure_monitor). This handles:
- FastAPI HTTP auto-instrumentation
- Proper span export to App Insights
- LiveMetrics/QuickPulse
- Correct sampler configuration

Override OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER=always_on for demos to avoid 10% sampling.

- vultron/adapters/driving/fastapi/inbox_handler.py

fallback from six test files under test/core/use_cases/. Also update the
test mock in test/api/v2/backend/test_inbox_handler.py to accept dl kwarg.

refactor(tests): centralize _make_payload as shared fixture (TECHDEBT-36)

Remove duplicate local _make_payload() from 5 test files and replace
with the make_payload pytest fixture already in conftest.py.

- test_status_use_cases.py: 7 methods updated
- test_actor_use_cases.py: 13 methods updated
- test_note_use_cases.py: 6 methods updated
- test_case_use_cases.py: 6 methods updated
- test_embargo_use_cases.py: 11 methods updated

Also marks TECHDEBT-38 complete (outbox_handler early-return was already
present in code; plan entry not previously checked off).

985 tests pass.

feat: e2e trace demo with traceparent propagation and custom metadata headers

- Install langchain-azure-ai from upstream main
- Extract metadata-* HTTP headers and inject as gen_ai.custom.* metadata
  so the AzureAIOpenTelemetryTracer sets them as span attributes
  (visible as customDimensions in Azure Monitor)
- Remove connection_string gate from create_langchain_callbacks so the
  tracer works with any pre-configured TracerProvider
- Validated: 21 spans, 16/21 carry incoming traceparent, custom headers
  appear on 12 agent spans

commit.message:
feat: swipe gesture to navigate tabs on mobile

Detect horizontal touchstart/touchend on the app container.
Swipe left advances to next tab, swipe right goes back.
Minimum 50px horizontal threshold, ignores mostly-vertical gestures.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: show all standings columns on mobile with horizontal scroll

Remove col-hide-mobile display:none on mobile; reduce font size and
padding so all columns fit with existing overflow-x scroll.
